title: OCI transport misinterprets tag
body:
Manpage:
```
       oci:path:tag
         An image tag in a directory compliant with "Open Container Image Layout Specification" at path.
```

What I did:
```
$ skopeo copy docker-daemon:registry.access.redhat.com/rhscl/mariadb-101-rhel7:latest oci:$PWD:asdqwe
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:8049b1db64b59b9aba69acd631e179b5888f15c0ad4147eacc76edad6f688728
 200.88 MB / 200.88 MB [===================================================] 13s
Copying blob sha256:f2baec9aa44a1f08c82f7d8dd4c3792ef1f128d33a4b0b17621b701b8c4192c2
 10.00 KB / 10.00 KB [======================================================] 0s
Copying blob sha256:f57403cba83946b0b2806b3fc5acbca44037e8813a2cd8638db8bd4001469897
 23.60 MB / 23.60 MB [======================================================] 4s
Copying blob sha256:761d6e9e006befde6e86306cf1efdbe083705865e5c45ef9d53efda041064b4f
 221.79 MB / 221.79 MB [===================================================] 13s
Copying config sha256:bff95184a80e19db4c523922383c25e2a1d08f5d9d7adab2a380e2c170aebdf9
 3.42 KB / 3.42 KB [========================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
```
I expected that the image will be present in `$PWD`:
```
$ ls -lha
total 0
```

But it's not. Instead `path` and `tag` were joined and that was the path skopeo used.

```
skopeo-0.1.31-5.git0144aa8.fc29.x86_64
```

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

I’m afraid I can’t reproduce this.  What is the _precise_ value of `$PWD`? (And in particular, does it contain any colons?)

It's indeed hard to reproduce. When I pick a different image or go to a different dir it works as expected.
```
$ skopeo copy docker-daemon:registry.access.redhat.com/rhscl/mariadb-101-rhel7:latest oci:$PWD:asdqwe
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:8049b1db64b59b9aba69acd631e179b5888f15c0ad4147eacc76edad6f688728
 200.88 MB / 200.88 MB [===================================================] 13s
Copying blob sha256:f2baec9aa44a1f08c82f7d8dd4c3792ef1f128d33a4b0b17621b701b8c4192c2
 10.00 KB / 10.00 KB [======================================================] 0s
Copying blob sha256:f57403cba83946b0b2806b3fc5acbca44037e8813a2cd8638db8bd4001469897
 23.60 MB / 23.60 MB [======================================================] 1s
Copying blob sha256:761d6e9e006befde6e86306cf1efdbe083705865e5c45ef9d53efda041064b4f
 221.79 MB / 221.79 MB [===================================================] 12s
Copying config sha256:bff95184a80e19db4c523922383c25e2a1d08f5d9d7adab2a380e2c170aebdf9
 3.42 KB / 3.42 KB [========================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
$ ll
total 0
$ echo $PWD
/home/tt/t/skopeo
$ ll ../skopeosdqwe
total 8.0K
drwxr-xr-x. 3 tt tt  19 Jun 26 09:31 blobs
-rw-r--r--. 1 tt tt 235 Jun 26 09:31 index.json
-rw-r--r--. 1 tt tt  31 Jun 26 09:31 oci-layout

```

Are you using `zsh` by any chance?

```console
bash# (FOO=/home/tt/t/skopeo; echo $FOO:abcde)
/home/tt/t/skopeo:abcde

zsh# (FOO=/home/tt/t/skopeo; echo $FOO:abcde)
/home/tt/t/skopeobcde
zsh# (FOO=/home/tt/t/skopeo; echo ${FOO}:abcde)
/home/tt/t/skopeo:abcde
```

Or just confirm the result of the parameter expansion by adding an `echo␣` before the `skopeo copy…` command.
Damn, I would not expect `zsh` to be so tricky and having different syntax for `$FOO`. I learnt something today, thank you.

Also found this in the documentation if anyone runs into this:
[Parameter Expansion](http://zsh.sourceforge.net/Doc/Release/Expansion.html#Parameter-Expansion)
```
The braces are required if the expansion is to be followed by a letter, digit,
or underscore that is not to be interpreted as part of name. In addition, more
complicated forms of substitution usually require the braces to be present;
exceptions, which only apply if the option KSH_ARRAYS is not set, are a single
subscript or any colon modifiers appearing after the name, or any of the
characters ‘^’, ‘=’, ‘~’, ‘#’ or ‘+’ appearing before the name, all of which
work with or without braces. 
```
Would it make sense to point to this problem in documentation?
I’m not sure; I don’t think that mentioning this in _every_ place where we use colons as separators is reasonable, and mentioning it once somewhere at the top would probably not be that effective.
